introduction: As Lee's Market continues to expand, maintaining in-house cybersecurity resources becomes a challenging task. This presentation explores the potential benefits and improvements of adopting Managed Security Service Providers (MSSPs) to ensure robust threat management, compliance, and customer trust.

subsections.heading: Current Cybersecurity Resource Constraints
subsections.content:
• In May 2025, we encountered five security incidents outside of regular office hours. 
• Our team was only able to promptly address two of these incidents before the start of the next workday. 
• This situation underscores the limited capacity of our in-house resources to handle real-time threats, especially during non-business hours. 
• The remaining incidents were deferred, potentially exposing Lee's Market to increased risk of data breach and system compromise. 
• Given the high threat level in the retail sector, with 80% of retailers experiencing cyberattacks within the past year, this delay may significantly increase our vulnerability. 
• Such delays could also lead to regulatory fines, as experienced by 33% of retailers post cyberattack, and reputational damage, seen in 53% of cases. 
• A partnership with a Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) could offer dedicated resources to address these constraints, enhancing our capability to respond to threats in a timely manner. 
• Leveraging MSSPs could reduce our risk of operational and reputational exposure and increase customer trust by ensuring a secure shopping environment.
subsections.justification: This content highlights Lee's Market's current cybersecurity limitations and provides justification for the adoption of MSSPs to improve threat management, compliance, and customer trust.

subsections.heading: Potential Benefits of Adopting MSSPs
subsections.content:
• MSSPs typically have a broader view of the threat landscape due to their work with multiple clients.
• Outsourcing could potentially free up internal resources to focus on strategic initiatives.
• MSSPs usually offer 24/7 monitoring services, which could enhance our incident detection and response capabilities.
• There's potential for cost savings if we can negotiate a favorable contract with an MSSP.
• We might gain access to advanced security technologies that would be too expensive to implement in-house.
• MSSPs can provide regular reports and metrics to demonstrate their effectiveness and value.
subsections.justification: This slide describes general benefits of MSSPs but doesn't specify how they can be leveraged to address Lee's Market's resource constraints.

subsections.heading: Expected Improvements in Threat Management and Compliance
subsections.content:
• Enhanced threat intelligence could lead to faster detection of potential attacks.
• We could potentially achieve higher compliance rates with industry and regulatory standards.
• Improved incident response capabilities could minimize the impact of security breaches.
• The use of MSSPs might enhance our PCI DSS compliance performance.
• More efficient threat management could result in fewer disruptions to business operations.
• Our ability to demonstrate strong cybersecurity measures could potentially increase customer trust.
subsections.justification: This slide outlines potential improvements but doesn't provide measurable data or compare MSSPs to in-house approaches.

conclusion: Adopting MSSPs can provide a scalable solution to our growing cybersecurity needs, improving our threat management, compliance, and customer trust. Let's explore potential providers and plan a phased transition to ensure uninterrupted security coverage.
